ownership.name: John Mara and Steve Tisch (co-owners)
ownership.style: Legacy ownership; loyal to staff but willing to make major coaching hires; patience wears thin after sustained losing
ownership.tendencies: Hired John Harbaugh despite other openings available; shows commitment to franchise-changing hires

gm.name: Joe Schoen
gm.tenure: 5th year (hired 2022); power has shifted — must work directly with Harbaugh
gm.philosophy: Aggressive roster construction around Jaxson Dart; willing to trade down from #5 to acquire more picks (only two top-100 selections currently)
gm.analytics_approach: Hybrid — modern front office sensibilities paired with traditional scouting

head_coach.name: John Harbaugh
head_coach.tenure: 1st year (hired January 20, 2026 — 21st head coach in Giants history)
head_coach.scheme_offense: Versatile system designed around Jaxson Dart; Harbaugh compared Dart to Lamar Jackson on film; strong run-game foundation
head_coach.scheme_defense: Multiple front; aggressive blitz packages; Baltimore-style physical defense

decision_makers.primary: Joe Schoen (GM) and John Harbaugh (HC) — collaborative with Harbaugh holding significant influence

roster_2026.qb_situation: Jaxson Dart is the franchise QB (2025 draft pick); Harbaugh's #1 reason for choosing Giants; second-year development critical
key_signings: Isaiah Likely (TE, from Ravens followed Harbaugh)
expiring_contracts: Malik Nabers (WR — coming off torn ACL in Week 4 of 2025, lost year after great rookie season)

needs.reason: Worst in NFL allowing 5.3 yards per carry; interior defensive line must be addressed for Harbaugh's physical defense
Protecting and developing Jaxson Dart is top priority; Harbaugh wants to build from the trenches
Malik Nabers recovering from torn ACL; need reliable targets while Nabers gets healthy; only two top-100 picks limits options
